reasoning: The development and approval of a new vaccine is a process that takes many years. Recent scientific literature from June 2025 confirms that there are currently no licensed vaccines available for Ureaplasma urealyticum, and current research is merely at the 'reverse vaccinology' (in-silico/computational) stage. Furthermore, as an easily treatable bacterial infection using standard antibiotics, there is no emergency clinical push that would radically accelerate this timeline. Given that the forecast horizon is only 1-2 months, it is functionally impossible for a legitimate vaccine to be created, tested, licensed, and updated on the 'List of infectious diseases' Wikipedia page by February or March 2026. The only way this resolves 'Yes' would be persistent, uncorrected vandalism on the Wikipedia page on the exact resolution dates, which is highly improbable. Therefore, the probabilities are set to the lowest highly confident baseline (increasing slightly over time to account for any random Wikipedia modifications over a longer horizon).
